req.title: Beneficiary management (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-036 governs beneficiary creation with a 24-hour cooling-off period. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive — a valid, unique beneficiary is saved in 'pending' state; (2) NEG negative — a duplicate account number returns HTTP 409; (3) BVA boundary — an account number at the exact maximum allowed length (e.g. 34 chars per IBAN spec) is accepted and enters pending state; (4) STC state-transition — a beneficiary in 'pending' state cannot receive a transfer before the 24-hour window elapses. Each test is idempotent via unique account-number generation (UUID-seeded) and isolated fixture teardown.

tests.name: test_add_valid_beneficiary_enters_pending_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
Account number used is not already registered for this customer
actions: Generate a unique account number via <unique_account_number> helper
POST /beneficiaries with valid payload containing the unique account number
expected: Response body contains a non-null 'cooling_off_until' timestamp 24 h in the future
trace: R-FIN-036: beneficiary saved in pending state
R-FIN-036: 24-hour cooling-off period applied on creation
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_add_valid_beneficiary_enters_pending_state(auth_token, api_client, unique_account_number, cleanup_beneficiary):
    account = unique_account_number(length=16)
    payload = {
        "account_number": account,
        "account_name": "Jane Doe",
        "bank_code": "TESTBANK"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"})
    cleanup_beneficiary(account)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    cooling_off = datetime.fromisoformat(body["cooling_off_until"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    assert cooling_off > now + timedelta(hours=23, minutes=59)
    assert cooling_off <= now + timedelta(hours=24, minutes=1)

tests.name: test_add_duplicate_beneficiary_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
A beneficiary with the target account number already exists for this customer
actions: Generate a unique account number via <unique_account_number> helper
POST /beneficiaries with the account number (first creation — expected to succeed)
POST /beneficiaries again with the identical account number (duplicate attempt)
Capture the HTTP response of the second request
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating duplicate beneficiary
trace: R-FIN-036: Error pathway — 409 if duplicate account
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_add_duplicate_beneficiary_returns_409(auth_token, api_client, unique_account_number, cleanup_beneficiary):
    account = unique_account_number(length=16)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    payload = {
        "account_number": account,
        "account_name": "John Smith",
        "bank_code": "TESTBANK"
    }

    first = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, "Setup failed: first creation should succeed"
    cleanup_beneficiary(account)

    second = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 409
    body = second.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DUPLICATE_BENEFICIARY"

tests.name: test_add_beneficiary_at_max_account_number_length
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
API accepts account numbers up to 34 characters (IBAN max per ISO 13616)
actions: Construct an account number of exactly 34 alphanumeric characters (max boundary)
POST /beneficiaries with this max-length account number
expected: Stored 'account_number' in response equals the 34-character input exactly
trace: R-FIN-036: Boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_ACCOUNT_LEN = 34

def test_add_beneficiary_at_max_account_number_length(auth_token, api_client, cleanup_beneficiary):
    account = "A" * MAX_ACCOUNT_LEN
    assert len(account) == MAX_ACCOUNT_LEN
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    payload = {
        "account_number": account,
        "account_name": "Max Boundary",
        "bank_code": "TESTBANK"
    }

    resp = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload, headers=headers)
    cleanup_beneficiary(account)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert body["account_number"] == account

tests.name: test_pending_beneficiary_cannot_receive_transfer_during_cooling_off
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
A newly created beneficiary exists in 'pending' state (cooling-off not yet elapsed)
actions: Create a new beneficiary via POST /beneficiaries; assert 201 and status=='pending'
Immediately attempt a transfer to that beneficiary via POST /transfers
Capture the HTTP response of the transfer attempt
expected: Transfer POST returns HTTP 422 or 403 (business rule rejection)
Response body error indicates beneficiary is in cooling-off period
trace: R-FIN-036: 24-hour cooling-off period — beneficiary cannot receive transfers until period elapses
State transition: pending -> (no transfer allowed) -> active
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_pending_beneficiary_cannot_receive_transfer_during_cooling_off(
    auth_token, api_client, unique_account_number, cleanup_beneficiary
):
    account = unique_account_number(length=16)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    bene_payload = {
        "account_number": account,
        "account_name": "Cooling Off Test",
        "bank_code": "TESTBANK"
    }
    bene_resp = api_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=bene_payload, headers=headers)
    assert bene_resp.status_code == 201
    bene_id = bene_resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_beneficiary(account)

    transfer_payload = {
        "beneficiary_id": bene_id,
        "amount": "10.00",
        "currency": "GBP",
        "reference": "cooling-off-test"
    }
    transfer_resp = api_client.post("/transfers", json=transfer_payload, headers=headers)

    assert transfer_resp.status_code in (403, 422)
    body = transfer_resp.json()
    assert "cooling" in str(body).lower() or "pending" in str(body).lower()
